title: Cyclosorus morobensis
taxonomy: Cyclosorus morobensis is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the 'broad buckler ferns'. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns that are typically found in tropical regions around the world. Cyclosorus morobensis is native to Papua New Guinea and is a relatively rare species. It is a delicate, shade-loving plant that thrives in humid environments.

intro: Cyclosorus morobensis is a species of fern, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta, characterized by its leafy fronds and reproductive structures. Traditionally, Cyclosorus morobensis is used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyclosorus morobensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the production of capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
